authorized: migrations on disposable or local data
review and release actions needed to finish this repository
excluded: deployment to an unnamed external production host

accepted_deviations.title: Navigation grouping
accepted_deviations.decision: Use Dashboard, AI Assets, Audit Reports, Knowledgebase, System, Users as the authenticated navigation group order.
compensating_controls: approval and audit controls remain enforced
contextual links preserve access to non-grouped workflows

accepted_deviations.title: Single-container co-location
accepted_deviations.decision: Use one application container and one application-data volume as the release delivery boundary.
compensating_controls: cgroup v2, seccomp, and AppArmor/SELinux verification
masked kernel interfaces, no setuid/ptrace, fresh run directories, cleanup, and startup refusal

security_remediation_records.resolution: Executable federation, durable session, MFA/step-up, immutable owner/dual approval, and fail-closed grant contracts are added.
security_remediation_records.evidence: Identity/session and engagement authorization negative-test records are retained in the API test evidence bundle.

security_remediation_records.resolution: Per-workload mTLS, envelope key lifecycle, ciphertext-only storage, one-use leases, and negative trust tests are added.
security_remediation_records.evidence: Workload identity, envelope-secret, and worker-client negative-test records are retained with redacted payload inspection.

security_remediation_records.resolution: Authenticated co-resident services, per-run namespaces, cgroup/LSM/seccomp controls, masked interfaces, cleanup, and startup refusal are required.
security_remediation_records.evidence: Container supervision and sandbox containment negative-test output records namespace, resource, masked-path, and startup-refusal checks.

security_remediation_records.resolution: Signed keyed checkpoints to separately administered external WORM/Object-Lock storage and legal-hold denial tests are required.
security_remediation_records.evidence: Evidence checkpoint, legal-hold, and infrastructure negative-test records cover mutation, truncation, replay, and admin-bypass denial.

security_remediation_records.resolution: Typed tool boundaries, DLP before persistence/egress, quarantine, and adversarial prompt-injection fixtures are required.
security_remediation_records.evidence: Typed-adapter, parser DLP, and API DLP negative-test records cover injection, redirect, smuggling, oversize, and detector-outage cases.

security_remediation_records.resolution: Fail-closed signer/trust-root/provenance/revocation/anti-rollback verification is required and mutable-main updates are unavailable.
security_remediation_records.evidence: Release artifact verification records reject unsigned, tampered, wrong-signer, revoked, stale, and older-version candidates.

security_remediation_records.resolution: Encrypted access-separated off-volume backups, consistency ordering, RPO/RTO, and fresh-volume recovery drills are required.
security_remediation_records.evidence: Backup/restore and evidence-checkpoint negative-test records cover total-volume-loss reconciliation, key/config recovery, legal holds, and checkpoint verification.

requirements.statement: Production Option 2 is one deployable application container containing web, API, PostgreSQL, Redis, MinIO/object storage, Node worker, and Python worker processes.
requirements.evidence: production Compose config, image digest, service-count contract output

requirements.statement: PID 1 is a fixed supervisor with declared process executables, users, readiness checks, restart behavior, and logs.
requirements.evidence: supervisor manifest, process health output, restart/failure-injection log

requirements.statement: PostgreSQL, Redis, object storage, and evidence data persist in dedicated subdirectories of one named volume rooted at /var/lib/shore-sentinel, with backup and restore procedures.
requirements.source: architecture_spec:4; plan:Migration and data-safety contract; plan:Task 3
requirements.evidence: volume inventory, pg_dump/Redis/MinIO backup records, restore rehearsal

requirements.statement: Processes have logical least-privilege boundaries through users, ownership, environment allowlists, loopback bindings, capability restrictions, and read-only paths.
requirements.evidence: non-root/process-user inventory, environment diff, capability and mount inspection

requirements.statement: Option 1 remains a local one-time audit with local evidence and no implicit enrollment or upload.
requirements.evidence: reviewed local-audit command and redacted local output

requirements.statement: Option 2 builds and runs the single-container application with one persistent application-data volume and documented health checks.
requirements.evidence: staging install log, health output, stop-without-volume-deletion record

requirements.statement: Authenticated navigation uses the approved ordered groups Dashboard, AI Assets, Audit Reports, Knowledgebase, System, Users.
requirements.evidence: navigation contract test output and three-viewport browser captures

requirements.statement: Versioned SQL migrations run once under an advisory lock before application processes; API startup does not mutate schema or recompute seed hashes.
requirements.evidence: ordered migration log, advisory-lock contention output, startup DDL-negative test

requirements.statement: Migration upgrade, fresh install, interruption, restart, and rollback compatibility are tested against existing data contracts.
requirements.source: architecture_spec:7; plan:Task 4; plan:Migration and data-safety contract
requirements.evidence: fresh/upgrade/interruption/restart/rollback migration records

requirements.statement: Production secrets are required and are not exposed to browser clients, queue payloads, logs, or unauthorized processes.
requirements.evidence: redacted secret scan, environment allowlist, payload inspection

requirements.statement: Node and Python workers use separate authenticated workload paths, and Python cannot reach managed-target egress.
requirements.evidence: positive/negative worker identity and parser egress test output

requirements.statement: Independent host/network default-deny egress or authenticated gateway enforcement remains required; application validation is not firewall proof.
requirements.evidence: check-only policy result plus approved external positive/negative fixture evidence

requirements.statement: Disposable AI test bundles use bounded unprivileged OS process/user/namespace sandboxes inside the one application container; no second runner host/container or Docker socket is part of the release baseline.
requirements.evidence: sandbox policy/process inspection, positive and negative sandbox matrix, no-socket mount inspection

requirements.statement: Existing SSH host-key, root/path/CIDR, ForceCommand, bounded cancellation, and one-time grant controls remain mandatory.
requirements.evidence: approved disposable fixture protocol output and negative SSH matrix

requirements.statement: Artifacts and audit events carry hashes, provenance, policy/bundle/image identity, parser version, retention, and append-only linkage.
requirements.evidence: provenance manifest, hash-chain verification, retention/legal-hold output

requirements.statement: Health reflects all required process/dependency readiness and reports degraded state instead of unconditional green.
requirements.evidence: healthy and dependency-failure health outputs with timestamps

requirements.statement: Updates validate source/config, backup persistent state, migrate once, record identity, and support rollback.
requirements.source: architecture_spec:7; architecture_spec:11; plan:Task 7; plan:Migration and data-safety contract
requirements.evidence: staged update and rollback rehearsal, backup IDs, prior-image health check

requirements.statement: The image uses pinned runtime/package inputs, the root lockfile, SBOM generation, and retained image digest.
requirements.evidence: Dockerfile contract, SBOM, dependency audit, image digest

requirements.statement: Navigation remains accessible and responsive with the approved labels and required interaction states.
requirements.evidence: browser viewport captures, accessibility report, console/page/overflow record

requirements.statement: The release has executable unit, contract, structural, Compose, migration, security-negative, browser, backup/restore, and rollback coverage.
requirements.evidence: CI run URL/ID, command logs, browser evidence, restore/rollback records

requirements.statement: Supervisor, API, worker, migration, and storage events are structured, redacted, timestamped, and reviewable.
requirements.evidence: redacted structured log bundle and observability review

requirements.statement: No unmet control is silently waived; any exception records scope, expiry, compensating control, and approver.
requirements.evidence: complete evidence index and signed waiver records, or explicit no-waiver result

requirements.statement: Candidate-specific evidence scores at least 95/100 and meets every category threshold and mandatory release gate.
requirements.evidence: reviewed scorecard with category points, threshold result, candidate commit, and image digest

requirements.statement: Prior delivery/configuration and persistent-data backups remain usable until staged rollback passes.
requirements.source: architecture_spec:11; plan:Migration and data-safety contract; plan:Rollback and review boundary
requirements.evidence: prior image/config identity, volume backup references, staged rollback health and critical-flow result

requirements.statement: Human federation, durable sessions, MFA/step-up, immutable owner authorization, dual approval, and execution grants are validated and fail closed.
requirements.evidence: issuer/JWKS/audience/nonce/state/PKCE negatives, session TTL/rotation/revocation, ACR/AMR step-up, owner/dual-approval, scope/expiry/revocation/policy-hash grant records
negative_test_artifacts: api/test/identity-session.test.ts: rejects wrong issuer, JWKS, audience, nonce, state, PKCE, and stale step-up claims
api/test/engagement-authorization.test.ts: rejects missing dual approval, expired/revoked grants, scope escape, and policy-hash drift

requirements.statement: Workload mTLS trust and envelope-encrypted target-secret delivery enforce certificate/key lifecycle, audience, rotation, and one-use attempt-bound leases.
requirements.evidence: trust-store/SAN/audience/expiry/revocation negatives, key-version rotation, ciphertext-only persistence, replay/cross-workload/KMS-outage denial, redacted payload scan
negative_test_artifacts: api/test/workload-identity.test.ts: rejects invalid SAN, audience, expiry, revocation, and cross-workload certificates
api/test/envelope-secret.test.ts: rejects plaintext persistence, replay, wrong key version, and KMS outage
workers/worker-node/test/apiClient.test.js: rejects missing, reused, and cross-workload attempt-bound leases

requirements.statement: Co-resident services and disposable runs have authenticated least-privilege boundaries, mandatory namespace/resource/LSM controls, and readiness refusal when containment primitives are unavailable.
requirements.evidence: DB/Redis/MinIO role inspection, namespace/cgroup/seccomp/LSM/masked-path checks, cross-process/signal/ptrace/symlink/resource negatives, startup refusal output
negative_test_artifacts: tests/test_container_supervision_contract.py: rejects missing process users, leaked environment values, writable paths, and unavailable readiness dependencies
tests/test_sandbox_containment.py: rejects missing namespaces, cgroup/LSM/seccomp controls, ptrace, symlink escape, and resource limits
workers/worker-node/test/sandbox.test.js: rejects cross-process access and reports startup refusal when containment primitives are unavailable

requirements.statement: Evidence hash chains emit signed keyed monotonic checkpoints to separately administered external WORM/Object-Lock storage, and legal holds cannot be bypassed.
requirements.evidence: external checkpoint receipt, signer/key custody record, mutation/truncation/replay/clock-skew/restore mismatch and hold-delete/admin-bypass negatives
negative_test_artifacts: api/test/evidence-checkpoint.test.ts: rejects mutation, truncation, replay, clock skew, restore mismatch, and non-monotonic checkpoints
api/test/legal-hold.test.ts: rejects hold-protected deletion and administrator bypass
tests/test_infrastructure_release_evidence.py: rejects missing external WORM receipt, external key custody, and signed checkpoint evidence

requirements.statement: Untrusted model, retrieval, tool, and external content cannot become supervisor/policy instructions; typed adapters enforce DLP and quarantine before persistence or egress.
requirements.evidence: typed-schema and fixed-adapter output, DLP/quarantine records, indirect/encoded/redirect/argument-smuggling/oversize/detector-outage negatives
negative_test_artifacts: workers/worker-python/test/test_dlp_and_injection.py: rejects indirect and encoded injection, oversized payloads, and detector outage without quarantine
workers/worker-node/test/toolAdapter.test.js: rejects malicious redirects, argument smuggling, and unallowlisted destinations or secret references
api/test/dlp-gate.test.ts: rejects persistence or egress before DLP and requires quarantine on detector failure

requirements.statement: Supply-chain and update admission fails closed on missing or invalid signatures, trust roots, provenance, revocation, or anti-rollback policy; mutable-main updates are unavailable until verified.
requirements.evidence: pinned signer/trust-root and provenance/SBOM records, tampered/unsigned/wrong-signer/revoked/stale/old-version denials, explicit unavailable-path review
negative_test_artifacts: tests/test_release_artifact_verification.py: rejects unsigned, tampered, wrong-signer, revoked, stale, and older-than-approved release candidates

requirements.statement: Independent encrypted off-volume recovery preserves consistency, keys/config recovery, RPO/RTO, retention/legal holds, and signed evidence checkpoints after total volume loss.
requirements.source: architecture_spec:5.4; architecture_spec:10; plan:SC-031; plan:Migration and data-safety contract
requirements.evidence: encrypted off-volume backup ID, quiesce/checkpoint manifest, recoverable KMS/config identity, RPO/RTO, fresh-empty-volume restore reconciliation, legal-hold and checkpoint verification
negative_test_artifacts: tests/test_backup_restore_contract.py: rejects in-volume or unencrypted backups, missing key/config identity, inconsistent quiesce ordering, and unreconciled fresh-volume restores
api/test/evidence-checkpoint.test.ts: rejects recovery when signed evidence checkpoints, retention, or legal holds do not reconcile
